task: Create a premium FrankX editorial article for the best audiophile wireless earbuds in 2026, including current research, ranked recommendations, first-principles hardware reasoning, user-review synthesis, AI-assistant prompts, and a local hero asset.
audience: Frank, premium audio buyers, Android power users, AirPods switchers, and creator-operator readers who value practical engineering judgment.
first_read: Frank should buy Sony WF-1000XM6 first unless his phone ecosystem makes Samsung, Huawei, or AirPods the stronger daily system.

references.label: Sony WF-1000XM6 specifications and reviews
references.reason: Baseline winner: codec support, driver data, battery, ANC, and broad Android-neutral feature set.

references.label: Technics EAH-AZ100 product and reviews
references.reason: Engineering-value counterpick: magnetic fluid driver, LDAC, LC3, battery, multipoint.

references.label: Bowers & Wilkins Pi8 reviews
references.reason: Luxury-sound counterpick: carbon cone driver, aptX Lossless path, premium tuning trade-offs.

references.label: Samsung Galaxy Buds4 Pro
references.reason: Galaxy ecosystem pick: two-way speaker, dual amps, UHQ codec constraints.

references.label: Huawei FreeBuds Pro 5
references.reason: Most ambitious hardware/codec story and clearest ecosystem-lock caution.

references.label: OnePlus Buds 4
references.reason: Price-performance benchmark with dual drivers, dual DACs, LHDC 5.0, and strong ANC specs.

references.label: AirPods Pro 3
references.reason: UX baseline for Frank because he liked AirPods; iPhone-first ecosystem comparison.

keep: Direct FrankX voice: confident, technical, practical, not guru-coded.
Clear first-screen buying decision before detailed analysis.
Spec comparisons tied to actual phone compatibility, not marketing maximums.
A sense of gratitude for the hardware builders without vague spirituality.
avoid: Unverified superlatives or present-tense claims without source links.
Custom UI code when existing MDX components already solve the surface.
direction.signature: A first-principles earbud system lens: driver plus chamber plus seal plus DSP plus codec plus app plus reliability.
system: Existing FrankX MDX blog renderer, schema frontmatter, article hero image, tables, FAQ, and related internal links.
Hand-authored SVG hero with engineered earbuds, signal paths, and measurement rings.
No visible instructional UI text in the hero asset.
Source-linked article body with a prompt pack for future AI-assisted buying decisions.

artifacts.notes: MDX frontmatter, article body, FAQ, internal links, sources, ranking tables, and prompts were inspected.
SVG source inspected for dimensions, accessible title/description, no visible copy, no external assets, and stable 16:9 composition.
Evidence manifest conforms to validate_design_evidence.py requirements.

checks.name: research-currentness
checks.evidence: Used June 2026 official specs, current review pages, current best-of guides, and YouTube comparisons.

checks.name: content-schema
checks.evidence: Article includes title, description, date, author, category, tags, image, keywords, tldr, schema, FAQ, and readingGoal.

checks.name: first-principles-ranking
checks.evidence: Ranking model uses driver architecture, codec compatibility, seal, DSP, app, battery, call quality, and reliability.

checks.name: visual-qa
checks.evidence: Hero source has stable viewBox, responsive 16:9 framing, no external dependencies, no overlapping text, and a premium technical visual direction.

checks.name: frankx-voice
checks.evidence: Copy is direct, technical, buyer-useful, and avoids banned AI-slop phrases in the new article.

checks.name: blast-radius
checks.evidence: Implementation adds only article, hero SVG, and evidence JSON; no app code or shared renderer changes.

score.notes: L99 scoring: high editorial/research quality, strong source traceability, scoped implementation, premium but intentionally restrained local SVG hero. Two points reserved for post-deploy browser inspection.

known_risks: Earbud releases and firmware change quickly; article is date-stamped June 26, 2026.
Ecosystem codec support depends on exact phone model, operating system version, region, and firmware.
Fit is personal; final recommendation requires Frank's 72-hour ear test.
